subject: devin-jatho
object: 270K followers, 33.5M views, 260K avg views per video
text: I have 270,000 followers on Instagram, 33.5 million views across 127 videos, averaging 260,000 views per video

object: 0 to 130K followers in 5 months

text: Brian Mark went from 20,000 to 85,000 followers in just six months while simultaneously scaling his revenue from $800,000 per month to $1,000,000 per month

subject: negativity
object: positivity in hooks
text: Negativity always wins. Negative hooks outperform positive hooks

subject: hook-expansion
object: filling first 3 seconds with core promise
text: Hook expansion — you need to expand your hook so that it fills the entire first three seconds of your video with your core promise

subject: content-delivery
object: speaking in U form — addressing the viewer directly
text: Speaking in U form — always address the viewer directly, say 'you' not 'people' or 'they'

subject: recording-quality
object: credibility — poor quality kills perceived authority
text: Recording like a G — your video quality signals your credibility; poor recording quality kills perceived authority

subject: instagram-captions
object: keywords, hashtags, and clear call to action
text: Optimize captions — your caption is searchable, use keywords and a clear call to action

subject: content-bible-program
object: media starter kit, content principles, market research, viral formula, finding editor, growth hackers, monetization
text: The Content Bible entails the media starter kit, content creation principles, content market research, creating a viral video, finding an editor, growth hackers, and monetizing your audience

subject: personal-brand
object: evergreen asset that pays dividends for the rest of time
text: Build an evergreen personal brand that will pay you dividends for the rest of time

subject: devin-jatho
object: 320K followers, 488K avg views, never under 100K views in 62 consecutive weeks
text: I have 320,000 followers, 488,000 average views per video, and I have never posted a video that got under 100,000 views in 62 consecutive weeks

subject: media-launchpad
object: entry-level program designed to take you from zero to 10,000 followers
text: I just launched my new program called Media Launchpad, designed to take you from zero to 10,000 followers

subject: foundations
object: the most important portion of content creation education
text: I believe foundations is the most important portion of this course

subject: 5-quick-wins
object: negativity wins, hook expansion, U-form speaking, recording quality, caption optimization
text: The five quick wins: negativity always wins, hook expansion, speaking in U form, recording like a G, optimize captions

subject: personal-brand-building
object: willingness to do what very few are willing to do
text: A lot of people would say yes they are committed to building a personal brand but very few are willing to do what it takes

subject: scripting
object: consistent virality — not throwing stuff at the wall
text: If you want to consistently go viral on social media and not just throw stuff at the wall, then you need to script your videos

subject: devin-jatho
object: scripting toolkit into 5 softwares after writing 7,000 scripts
text: 7,000 scripts later, I've distilled my scripting toolkit into five softwares

subject: dovetail
object: transcribing top creators' videos to find script patterns
text: Tool number one is Dovetail — I use it to transcribe videos from top creators in any niche to study their script patterns

subject: sort-reels-extension
object: sorting a creator's Instagram by most-viewed to find viral patterns
text: I use a Chrome extension called Sort Reels that sorts a creator's Instagram account by most viewed, so I can find the patterns in their highest performing scripts

subject: scripting-research-workflow
object: view sorter to find top videos + Dovetail to transcribe + pattern extraction
text: The workflow is: use the view sorter to find a creator's most viewed videos, transcribe them with Dovetail, extract the patterns, and apply them to your own scripts

subject: scripting-research
object: studying Brandon Carter and Alex Hormozi to script Brian Mark's 4M+ view video
text: I studied Brandon Carter and Alex Hormozi's top performing content and used those patterns to script Brian Mark's video that got over 4 million views

subject: hooks
object: the single most critical lever for getting more views
text: If you want to get more views on your videos, you need to create better hooks

subject: cost-narration-hook
object: increasing perceived value by highlighting time or money invested to acquire knowledge
text: Cost Narration — whenever you increase the perceived value of the information you're going to share in your video by either highlighting the time, money, or both that you invested to gain the knowledge

subject: cost-narration-example
object: 'This weekend I flew to Chicago and spent $20,000 at the Alex Hormozi event — here are my top three takeaways'
text: This weekend I flew all the way to Chicago and spent close to $20,000 to have been at the Alex Hormozi event, and these are my top three valuable takeaways

subject: cost-narration
object: generic tip to $20K worth of wisdom — raises perceived video value
text: For the viewer, it now feels like it's a $20,000 video. Your hook just miraculously goes from here's a tip to here's 1,000 speeches worth of wisdom distilled down into a singular ritual

subject: train-bloom-framework
object: creator averaging 3.8M views per video across last 35 videos
text: Hook framework number two, the Train Bloom Framework. Within the past 35 videos that this guy's posted to his account, he has averaged 3.8 million views

subject: cost-narration-with-time
object: conditional tip to experiential authority signal
text: Instead of 'if you get nervous on stage right before every speech, always do this ritual' you would swap that for 'if you have anxiety when you have to present or speak in front of a large audience, this will change your life — I've given almost a thousand speeches within my lifetime'

subject: cost-narration-combined-example
object: 'I've visited over 100 countries, spent over $100,000 on travel in the last decade — here are my five hidden gems'
text: I've visited over 100 countries, spent over $100,000 on travel in the last decade, and here are my five hidden gems for your family to visit that you will not find in any travel guide

subject: cost-narration-hook
object: it instantly makes video perform better and perceived as more valuable
text: It will instantly make your video perform better and make your video perceived as more valuable

subject: devin-jatho
object: Austin TX penthouse floor 441 at $10,781/month, moved in at age 20
text: I'm 21 years old. Well, I got on our 20. This is my penthouse. Floor 441. $10,781 a month

subject: devin-jatho
object: pre-prepared meal delivery Snap Kitchen — doesn't cook
text: I order food from this company called Snap Kitchen. Outside of that, that's all I eat. I don't cook at all

subject: devin-jatho
object: 7-figure income before age 21 funding $10K/month penthouse via organic content
text: I'm 21 years old living in a penthouse at $10,781 a month — this is what building a personal brand can do

subject: devin-jatho
object: walk, ideation call, sales prep, sales meeting, parliament, Nexus mastermind, gym
text: 7:30 to 8 I go on a walk. 8 to 9 is the ideation call where my editors show me storyboards. 9 to 9:30 is sales team prep. 9:30 to 10 is the sales team meeting. 10 to 11 is what I call parliament — the full company meeting. 11 to 12 is the Nexus mastermind call, which is my favorite call of the week

subject: devin-jatho-company
object: dedicated editors, sales team, and parliament company-wide meeting structure
text: My company runs a daily ideation call with editors, a dedicated sales team with prep and meeting blocks, and a company-wide parliament meeting

subject: devin-jatho
object: highly structured daily operating schedule with no wasted time blocks
text: POV: you're 21, make 7 figures — this is the daily schedule that makes it possible

subject: devin-jatho
object: over 47 business owners grow to 100K+ followers and at minimum double monthly revenue
text: I've helped over 47 business owners grow over 100,000 followers and at the bare minimum double their monthly revenue

subject: nexus-program
object: private video review portal with weekly second-by-second personalized breakdowns
text: Inside Nexus there's a private video review portal where I give weekly personalized video breakdowns second-by-second

subject: hook-mistakes
object: view count before video has a chance
text: There are hook mistakes that kill views before your video even has a chance — I review these second-by-second in my breakdowns

subject: algorithm-content
object: theoretical unless it comes directly from Instagram
text: Every single video about the algorithm that doesn't come from Instagram itself is theoretical

subject: not-going-viral
object: not understanding how the algorithm works, not by the algorithm itself
text: The reason you're not going viral on Instagram isn't because of the algorithm. It is simply because you do not understand how the algorithm works

subject: instagram-algorithm
object: a game with learnable rules — once you know the rules, winning becomes easy
text: The algorithm is a game. And once you learn the rules of the game, then winning the game becomes quite easy

subject: instagram
object: paid ads, so algorithm optimizes for watch time to keep users on platform
text: Instagram makes 99% of its revenue from paid ads. The algorithm optimizes for watch time — if your content keeps people on the app, you are directly making Instagram money

subject: first-3-seconds
object: whether content makes Instagram money or costs Instagram money
text: If people are just scrolling past your videos in the first 3 seconds, then you're costing Instagram money

subject: algorithm-distribution
object: testing video with small interested group, comparing watch time, then amplifying winners
text: Whenever you post a video, Instagram doesn't blast it out to a million people. It tests your video with a small group of people that have already shown interest in the topic of your video

subject: virality
object: your video ranking highest in watch time among competing videos in the test batch
text: It's about how well your video performs within this test group in comparison to all of the other videos that the algorithm has to choose from. If your video ranks the highest, that is how you achieve virality

subject: instagram-competition
object: you vs every other creator the algorithm can choose from, not you vs the algorithm
text: This is not a game of you versus the algorithm. This is a game of you versus every other creator that the algorithm has to choose from

subject: client-jordan-airbnb-coach
object: $300K/month on $4K/day ads — target $600K/month without ads
text: Jordan is making $300,000 a month, spending $4,000 a day on ads, and the goal is to get him to $600,000 a month without any ads

subject: organic-content
object: bigger retargeting pool and higher cold ad conversion rates — compounds paid ad ROI
text: The direct correlation to making more money is just more warm leads. The organic content gives you a bigger retargeting opportunity and makes your cold ads convert at a higher rate

subject: income-claim-content
object: being perceived as another 20-year-old course seller teaching get-rich-quick
text: You don't want to come off as another 20-year-old course seller that's teaching people how to get rich online — that is the risk of income claims

subject: organic-content
object: the path to $600K/month by generating warm leads that reduce dependence on paid ads
text: The main focus in the business is more organic traffic and the way to get to $600,000 a month is going to be more organic traffic

subject: devin-jatho
object: 100+ business owners scaled to multiple 6-7 figures/month through organic
text: I have over 360,000 followers on Instagram and I've worked with over a 100 business owners to scale their online business to multiple six and seven figures per month through organic content

subject: coaching-client-diagnostic
object: increasing sales volume, optimizing offer stack, dialing in content pillars
text: number one, increasing sales volume. Number two, optimizing the offer stack. And then number three, just dialing in the content pillars

subject: low-ticket-community
object: ascend members to core high-ticket offer
text: you create a low ticket community and its sole purpose is to move them into your core offer and that is the only purpose of the low ticket community

subject: icp
object: specific named persona with clear pain point
text: you need to get very specific on your ICP. Who specifically is your offer for and what specific problem are you solving

subject: content-pillars
object: ICP pain points and desired outcomes
text: the content pillars need to reflect the exact problems and desires of your ideal client profile

subject: devin-jatho
object: 200K views — more than double nearest competitor
text: My past 50 videos on Instagram have averaged 200,000 views, more than double my nearest competitor

subject: trinity-of-value
object: Devin's flagship content model — the core of everything he teaches
text: Trinity of Value is...my flagship model...If I died today...I would give him this model

subject: trinity-of-value
object: clear solution, uniqueness, viewer depiction — pyramid with clear solution at base
text: Three components: clear solution, uniqueness, viewer depiction — arranged as a pyramid, clear solution at base

subject: video
object: the bridge from A (problem) to B (desired outcome)
text: your video must be the bridge in between A to B

subject: uniqueness
object: solution being easier, more effective, or better explained than what viewer already had
text: is your clear solution either easier, more effective, or better explained than the current solution that the viewer already had in their mind

subject: viewer-depiction
object: 3 belief questions: can they picture themselves doing it, will it work, is it worth the effort
text: can your viewer picture themselves taking the steps you gave in your video? Do they believe that those steps will work? And do they also believe that those steps are worth the effort?

subject: value-progression
object: get paid for what you do → what you know → who you are
text: First, you get paid for what you do. Second, you get paid for what you know. Third, you get paid for who you are

subject: agency-to-coaching-transition
object: hit $100K/mo with agency first, then switch to coaching
text: make 100k a month with the agency, then switch to coaching

subject: agency-without-systems
object: lack of client onboarding, delivery, and team operations systems
text: lack of systems for client onboarding, delivery, and team operations

subject: $7500-price
object: disgustingly low given the transformation delivered
text: the platinum high ticket was 7.5. I think that's disgustingly disgustingly low

subject: offer-stack-price-doubling
object: from $7.5K to $14K high-ticket price
text: we're going to change the price from 7.5 to 14. So, we're doubling the price on the high ticket

subject: follower-bottleneck
object: followers watching content but not converting to booked calls
text: he had followers that watched his content but just didn't turn into booked calls

subject: expert-problem
object: trying to cram complex ideas into short videos
text: every single video that he made, it just felt like he was cramming a textbook into 90 seconds

subject: 4-month-coaching-objectives
object: stabilize at $50K/mo, 2x IG views, clear content strategy, optimize offers
text: Number one is going to be consistently stabilized at 50K a month revenue. Number two is going to be to increase IG baseline to 10K views per post to 20K views per post. Number three is just making sure that we have a clear IG content strategy that is dialed in. And number four is to make sure that the offers are optimized

subject: devin-jatho-editing-style
object: 90% of competitors copy it and editing software named captions after him
text: 90% of my competitors are just carbon copies of me and my style. You can literally open up editing softwares nowadays and choose captions that are called the Devon captions

subject: hiring-world-class-editor
object: three phases: editor funnel, editor X-ray vision, payment incentive alignment
text: I'm going to guide you on how to make this happen in three simple but powerful phases. Starting with phase one, I'm going to help you build an editor funnel. Phase two, I'm going to equip you with editor X-ray vision. And then in phase three, we're going to go over payment

subject: good-editor
object: likelihood video goes viral — not just make it look cool
text: an editor that not only knows how to edit in a way that looks cool, but also increases the likelihood that your video goes viral

subject: editor-job-posting
object: authority statement, what we're looking for, benefits, should-not-apply filter, invitation
text: you start off with about us. Just make an elevator pitch about your company stating a clear authority statement and what role you're looking for. Then you create another section which is what we are looking for. For the benefits of joining a section, just list off the benefits. Then you go over the qualifications

subject: job-posting-filter-technique
object: framing positives to undesired crowd as negatives to desired crowd
text: you want to frame positives under you should not apply it. You want to frame things that would be seen as positives by the undesired crowd and then but also seen as negatives by the desired crowd

subject: devin-jatho
object: $160K/month revenue from purely organic — no ads, no cold outreach
text: No fucking ads. Only organic. Right now, I am projected for this month being the fourth of the month already at 106,000 for this month.

subject: organic-content
object: paid ads for business owners in both reach and ad performance
text: Everyone in the world that already cracked organic was making way more money organically since all free. Also on top of that, their ads perform better if they have a good organic.

subject: client-aayush
object: identity shift from videographer to content marketer
text: I was unable to stop being a videographer and start becoming a content marketer that could actually scale businesses organically.

subject: prior-coaches
object: cold DMs and ads without first cracking organic — hollow advice
text: All the other past mentorships, it was to either do a bunch of cold DMs, post content, but they never cracked the algorithm, so it was kind of empty like advice. Or run ads.

subject: ad-spend
object: organic content quality is poor — cost per client acquisition explodes
text: Their cost per client acquisition was just so horrible because their organic was so trash. So they would talk about how they're trying to keep their ad spend down and they're getting to worst months because well ad spend goes up when their content was also trash.

subject: minichat-new-follower-automation
object: new followers into leads and booked calls on autopilot
text: $67,492. That is how much I made in the past 4 days from this Minihat's automation. Instagram dropped a massive new update last week that basically allows you to be able to automatically message every single new follower that you get by using Minihat.

subject: new-follower-welcome-message
object: freebie that delivers the outcome they want
text: Hey, first name. I saw that you followed and I wanted to congratulate you on that decision with a free welcome gift. It's a freebie that will get you an outcome. The outcome that they want that you can drive.

subject: follower-automation-flow
object: freebie delivery → email gate → call booking
text: We found one clear winner that led to the most amount of emails collected, the most booked calls, and most importantly, the most sales.

subject: devin-jatho
object: makes multiple 7 figures entirely from organic content and helps online business owners do the same
text: My name is Devon Jathau. I make multiple seven figures entirely from organic content and I help online business owners do the same.

subject: nexus-coaching-program
object: $20K/month revenue to qualify
text: Sign up my highle program Nexus which if you make at least 20k per month you can learn more about within the first link in the description.

subject: content-growth-plan
object: find ideas → script them → record them → edit them
text: I'm going to be giving you guys a stepbystep action plan on how you guys can add 10,000 idol clients to your following list within as little as 90 days. And we're going to do this through four simple but powerful initiatives. With the first initiative being how to find content ideas, with the second initiative being how to script those said ideas, with the third initiative being how to record those said scripts, and then with the last initiative being how to edit those said recordings.

subject: credibility-claim
object: you've actually done it for yourself at the highest level
text: Almost everyone says that they'll help you build their personal brand, but not everyone has done it for themselves.

subject: narrow-problems
object: predictably creating viral content
text: The single most important factor for determining whether your video is going to go viral or not isn't the editing. It's not the video quality or even the script. I've realized that the key difference on how I predictably create viral content now everyone else tries to can be distilled down into one key idea. That key idea being my concept that I call narrow products.

subject: narrow-problem
object: specific problem enabling uniquely narrow solution delivering quick results
text: A narrower problem is also a narrow a more narrow solution. So, like if I'm saying how to lose 50 lbs in 30 days or something like that, like there it's going to be super generic because I can't get a super narrow solution. But if I narrow down the problem to like how to lose 5 lbs in 30 days, I can create a very narrow solution that's unique and that actually delivers the result quickly.

subject: devin-jatho
object: consistent 100K+ view floor for 922+ consecutive days on Instagram
text: This is my last video that I posted to Instagram that's gotten less than 100,000 views. And it was 922 days ago.

subject: viral-success
object: editing, video quality, script, luck, or voice — it's the problem framing
text: But the truth is, they're wrong. And if you think that, you're wrong. Because after working with over 200 different business owners and generating hundreds of millions of views, I've realized that the key difference on how I predictably create viral content now everyone else tries to can be distilled down into one key idea.

subject: nexus-coaching-call
object: $5K–$10K per hour based on client ROI
text: Assuming that I made this like a product or service, how much would you pay for one of these calls just for an hour of me going through the audiation? minimum of five grand because this is going to generate me probably 100 200 so minimum five probably more eight to 10.

subject: viral-video-bottleneck
object: generating ideas but knowing the direction to take them
text: After creating well over 500 videos that's gotten over a million views for myself and my clients, one of, if not the biggest bottlenecks that I know that business owners face that prohibits them from creating viral content is coming up with video ideas. However, not just video ideas themselves. It's easy to come up with video ideas, but the hard part is is coming up with the viral ideas and then understanding what direction you're supposed to take that set idea.

subject: interest-stacking
object: placing explosive moments every 5 segments to prevent viewer drop-off
text: Interest stacking where every five plays you add a not a high value play, but an explosive play that catches a coach's eyes so they don't you don't lose them throughout the video.

subject: viral-ideation
object: learning the lens — the thought process — not copying specific ideas
text: The value of this video is not going to be getting you ideas directly unless you just happen to be in the same niche as my client Troy. But what you will get is the ability to see what highlevel ideation extraction looks like. Be able to use that same process to generate an abundance of new viral ideas for yourself. And after seeing what you're about to see, you have an entirely different lens, an entirely new lens to look through whenever it comes down to coming up with viral ideas that you will not be able to unsee.

subject: devin-jatho
object: $41K from single Instagram Story campaign
text: I made $41,000 from this single story. Robin, one of my Nexus members, made $10–15k from it. The story got 43,000 views.

subject: talking-shit-story-template
object: fake skeptical comment + 3 client result photos + keyword CTA via MiniChat
text: Slide 1: Use a fake skeptical comment — 'no one is getting 10K followers in a month' — plus 'you sure?' and a photo pointing at the comment. Create the fake comment at easycomment.ai. Slide 2: Keep the fake comment at the top and add 3 photos of client results. Slide 3: 'These results aren't luck, we just optimized their process' plus a pitch and keyword CTA like 'reply with Nexus,' then use MiniChat to automate the DM.

subject: social-proof-stack
object: story hook — lead with skeptical doubt then demolish with evidence
text: The hook is a fake skeptical comment combined with three client result photos — it leads with doubt, then immediately demolishes it with evidence.

subject: devin-jatho
object: delivery matters more than script — you can steal a viral script and still fail
text: The key that everyone misses is HOW I say what I say — you can steal a viral script word for word and pay a high quality editor, but your video still will not perform if you cannot speak in a captivating way.

subject: devin-jatho
object: captivating delivery is learnable — not innate
text: You don't have to be a naturally gifted speaker, you just have to hold yourself to a higher standard and follow the process.

subject: recording-workflow
object: vibes run-through: loose script read + plan hand movements before actual take
text: Before recording I do a vibes run-through: read the script loosely and plan hand movements — so the actual recording is fluid and natural.

subject: devin-jatho
object: teleprompter with foot pedal for script scrolling while looking at camera
text: Setup: teleprompter plus a foot pedal to scroll the script — this lets me look directly at camera while reading without manual scrolling.

subject: devin-jatho
object: edgy disruptive first line over safe openers
text: Hook used: 'Instagram just slapped their dick on the table with this new update.' The first line of a video is critical — I chose this hook intentionally because it's edgy and disruptive.

subject: tier-list-hook-template
object: '[question using client language + desired result]? I have a tier list.'
text: Template: '[question using client language + desired result]? I have a tier list.' Example: 'Which business ideas are most likely to make you a millionaire? I have a tier list.' Use the exact language your ideal clients use — like 'nine-to-fivers who want to start a business.'

subject: damaging-admission
object: state a negative, add 'but', then add positive — negative makes positive more believable
text: Damaging admission: state a negative, add 'but', then add a positive. The negative makes the positive more believable. It's a classic copywriting technique applied to content.

subject: devin-jatho
object: ownership beats virality — millionaires buy cash-flowing businesses, not audiences
text: The millionaires I know aren't trying to go viral. They're buying businesses that cash flow on autopilot. You don't need to be a genius, you need ownership.

subject: expert-brand-vs-personal-brand
object: personal brand attracts followers, expert brand attracts buyers
text: I'm about to cross 500,000 followers on Instagram and I'm about to clear $5 million from my Instagram. But I didn't do it by building a personal brand. Personal brand attracts followers, an expert brand attracts buyers.

subject: know-like-trust
object: to selling — the only prerequisite is trust of expertise
text: Both roads personal brand builders go down fail because of a false belief: you need know/like/trust before people buy. But the only prerequisite to someone buying is trust of expertise. People don't need to like you, they need to trust your expertise.

subject: devin-jatho
object: neurosurgeon analogy — you pick who you trust, not who you like
text: Brain cancer — you don't pick a neurosurgeon based on whether he likes cats or dogs. You pick the one you trust as an expert. Buyers work the same way.

subject: devin-jatho
object: first paid for what you know, eventually for who you are — expertise must come first
text: First, you get paid for what you know, and then eventually you get paid for who you are. Hormozi didn't become Hormozi by talking about his calves — he built an expert brand first. Iman spent 4 years teaching agency building.

subject: expert-niche-selection
object: the problem your offer solves — broad enough for buyers, narrow enough to exclude non-buyers
text: Pick your expert niche defined by the problem your offer solves. Devin's: 'organic acquisition' not 'marketing.' Jeff Nippard's: 'science-based bodybuilding' not 'fitness.' Sweet spot: broad enough to include buyers, narrow enough to exclude non-buyers. Speak to one singular dream avatar.

subject: content-quadrants
object: 4 non-overlapping subtopics, minimum 6 videos/week, at least 1 per quadrant
text: Break your expert niche into 4 non-overlapping subtopics. Rules: no overlap between quadrants; each has a clear description. Post at least 6 videos/week with at least 1 per quadrant. Devin's 4: content strategy, monetization, engagement, video production. Hormozi's 4: persuasion tactics, pragmatic productivity, personal finance/wealth, business strategies.

subject: content-creator
object: becoming defined by accidental viral niche that diverges from core offer
text: Client made a recipe video go viral and doubled down on recipes — became 'the recipe guy' instead of the high-ticket fitness coach he intended to be. Don't follow virality that diverges from your expert niche.

subject: personal-brand-vs-expert-brand
object: entertainment (fragile) vs value delivery (compounds)
text: A personal brand depends on your ability to entertain, an expert brand depends on your ability to give value. Entertainment is fragile; expertise compounds.

subject: devin-jatho
object: age 19 as content creator — elevator pitch: 'we make millionaires famous on social media'
text: So the quick little elevator pitch that we like to say is we make millionaires famous on social media. You are right, I'm 19, I'm kind of young, but right now we're currently servicing entrepreneurs trying to help them build and monetize their personal brand.

subject: devin-jatho
object: Shark Tank episode where Kevin O'Leary asked about social media followers
text: I used to watch Shark Tank every single day and one day as a guy was coming up to pitch Kevin O'Leary, Mr. Wonderful asked how many social media followers do you have for the brand — and at that point it kind of stuck with me that this is important.

subject: devin-jatho
object: first viral video (1M views) on his 4th ever post
text: For me it's a little bit different because my fourth ever video got a million views, so it was like immediately I kind of got that first ever DM. The snowball effect where it was a normal occurrence was probably around two months into creating content.

subject: devin-jatho
object: entrepreneurial mindset since second grade — watching Shark Tank, selling things in school
text: I always had like the entrepreneur mindset since like second grade. I was always an advocate for watching Shark Tank. I was trying to sell things in school.

subject: devin-jatho
object: door-knocking solar sales job to go full-time in content — never looked back
text: I kind of quit the job that I had currently which was door knocking for solar — appointment setting — and then I just never looked back.

subject: devin-jatho
object: arrogant confidence — believes he is the best at everything
text: My mindset is very arrogant. I think I'm the best at everything. I am a prime advocate to not bitch your content. We're a different breed — action is what we got.

subject: devin-jatho
object: treating social media as a paid ad is the biggest mistake creators make
text: They treat social media as a sales like — it's a paid ad. That's probably the biggest mistake.

subject: bf-skinner-content-model
object: build positive reinforcement — education free, pay only for implementation
text: My whole ordeal is to build up enough positive reinforcement — like the BF Skinner experiment. I want my content to be the positive lever: if you see my page pop up on your feed, you at least know that you're going to get something positive from it. I talk about what I have to give — education — and then you can pay me if you want the implementation.

subject: devin-jatho

text: I feel like it's more so a common sense and then from that I'll try to reverse engineer the common sense with something to back it up. I'll go do research — ChatGPT is really good for this — to basically back my assumption and then make it somewhat of a fact.

text: I hate editing. So from day one I had a little guy that I knew from Fortnite previously and he wanted to edit the videos for $5 a video. Now we have five people on staff and we spend multi-five figures on the editing team.

subject: devin-jatho
object: brand quality matters more than appearance quality — brand is king
text: Brand is king. Everything comes a lot easier whenever you do have a brand. The quality of appearance is not the most important thing — it's the quality of content and then it's always the people that's actually consuming the content.

subject: devin-jatho
object: use back camera + 4K + most-compatible format on iPhone for best video quality
text: Use the back camera instead of the front — back camera is automatically better. On iPhone switch to 4K recording and go to camera settings, formats, and switch to most compatible — that'll make your video less blurry.
